cards.title: Google Photos Launches AI Video Remix Tool
cards.body: Google Photos introduces Video Remix, an AI feature that applies cinematic relighting, swaps backgrounds, and adds artistic styles to videos. The tool enhances dark clips and enables creative video editing directly in the app.

cards.title: Video Games Better Training Data Than Internet
cards.body: A CEO argues that video game data surpasses internet data for achieving AGI, as models like ChatGPT excel at text but struggle with spatial and temporal reasoning. Games provide superior training for understanding how objects move through space.

cards.title: Meta Adds Privacy Safeguard to AI Glasses
cards.body: Meta implements new safeguards to prevent secret recording with its AI glasses. The update conflicts with reports of the company expanding personal data collection for AI products.

cards.title: OpenAI Releases Advanced Voice Models
cards.body: OpenAI launches new voice models enabling simultaneous speaking and listening for natural conversations. The feature supports live translation and more intuitive voice interactions.

cards.title: Prime Intellect Raises $130M Series A
cards.body: Prime Intellect secures $130M Series A funding to help enterprises build proprietary AI agents. The startup aims to reduce organizational reliance on frontier AI labs.

cards.title: AI Startups Growing at Record Speeds
cards.body: Fast-growing AI startups are accelerating revenue growth at unprecedented rates. The sector continues to attract capital and talent at scale.

cards.title: Gaming Data Key to AGI Development
cards.body: A Bezos-backed startup argues gaming data is essential for AGI, as LLMs lack spatial-temporal reasoning. Video games provide superior training compared to internet text.

cards.title: Former OpenAI Exec Joins Stoke Space
cards.body: Kevin Weil, ex-OpenAI executive, joins the board of reusable rocket company Stoke Space. His appointment signals growing Silicon Valley interest in space technology.

cards.title: ZML Releases Free AI Inference Software
cards.body: French AI startup ZML, backed by Turing Award winner Yann LeCun, releases ZML/LLMD to reduce inference costs. The free software optimizes AI performance across multiple chips.

cards.title: SambaNova Raises $1B at $11B Valuation
cards.body: AI chip maker SambaNova reaches $11B valuation in new funding, just months after raising a mega round. The company's rapid growth surpasses prior Intel acquisition rumors.

cards.title: Meta Launches Muse Image Generator
cards.body: Meta releases Muse Image, a new AI image generator for advertising and creative applications. Users express concerns about unauthorized use of their photos in training.

cards.title: Open Source AI Growth Doesn't Hurt Anthropic
cards.body: Open source models and frontier labs capture different phases of the same AI lifecycle. Anthropic's success remains unimpacted by open source competition.

cards.title: Microsoft Cuts AI Spending With Own Models
cards.body: Microsoft joins industry trends by reducing AI spending and relying more on proprietary models. The shift reflects broader cost optimization across Silicon Valley.

cards.title: Discord Admits AI Moderation Bug Bans Users
cards.body: Discord confirms an AI moderation error wrongfully banned users over harmless images since May. The company fixed the bug after an additional 200 weekend bans.

cards.title: Claude Cowork Expands to Mobile and Web
cards.body: Anthropic's Claude Cowork now supports mobile and web platforms for seamless task continuity. Users can start work on desktop, check updates on phones, and continue later.

cards.title: Savi App Protects Against AI Voice Scams
cards.body: Savi raises $7M seed funding to combat realistic AI scams including deepfake kidnapping demands. The app launches Tuesday for iPhone and Android.

cards.title: American Autonomous Vehicles Fight in Ukraine
cards.body: Forterra deploys over 100 self-driving ATVs in Ukrainian conflict zones. The deployment marks significant adoption of autonomous ground vehicles in active combat.

cards.title: First AI Ransomware Attack Required Humans
cards.body: An AI agent executed a real ransomware attack's technical phase, but humans chose the victim and supplied credentials. The attack wasn't fully autonomous despite initial reports.

cards.title: SK Hynix Eyes US IPO Amid AI Boom
cards.body: Memory maker SK Hynix plans a multibillion-dollar US IPO Friday, capitalizing on AI-driven demand. The company rides the semiconductor boom to major market expansion.

cards.title: Vercel CEO Discusses Model-Agent Separation
cards.body: Vercel's Guillermo Rauch emphasizes price-performance optimization in separating AI models from agents. Production deployment requires rethinking how models and agents interact.

cards.title: Google Photos Adds AI Video Remix Tool
cards.body: Google's new AI feature applies cinematic relighting, swaps backgrounds, and adds artistic styles to videos. The tool enhances video content creation with intelligent visual transformations.

cards.title: Video Games Better Training Data Than Internet
cards.body: A CEO argues that gaming data surpasses internet text for achieving AGI. Large language models like ChatGPT excel at text but struggle understanding spatial and temporal movement.

cards.title: Meta AI Glasses Get New Privacy Safeguard
cards.body: Meta is adding protections to prevent secret recording with its AI glasses. The update arrives amid concerns about the company's expanding personal data collection for AI.

cards.title: OpenAI Releases Natural Voice Conversation Models
cards.body: OpenAI's new voice mode enables simultaneous speaking and listening for live translation. The advancement supports real-time conversational AI interactions.

cards.title: Prime Intellect Raises $130M for Enterprise AI
cards.body: Prime Intellect secured Series A funding to help organizations build their own agentic AI systems. The startup aims to reduce enterprise dependency on frontier AI labs.

cards.title: Fast-Growing AI Startups Accelerate Revenue Growth
cards.body: Several AI startups are experiencing unprecedented revenue acceleration. The sector shows increasingly rapid growth trajectories across multiple ventures.

cards.title: ZML Releases Free AI Inference Acceleration Software
cards.body: French AI startup ZML released ZML/LLMD to reduce AI inference costs across various chips. The software was endorsed by Turing Award winner Yann LeCun.

cards.title: SambaNova Raises $1B at $11B Valuation
cards.body: AI chip maker SambaNova achieved an $11 billion valuation in its latest funding round. The raise comes months after Intel reportedly considered acquiring the company.

cards.title: Meta Launches Muse Image AI Generator
cards.body: Meta released Muse Image for generating creative content in advertising and decoration. Users have raised concerns about the model's use of their photos.

cards.title: Open Source AI Growth Not Hurting Anthropic
cards.body: Open source AI models and frontier labs capture different phases of the AI lifecycle. Competition isn't eroding Anthropic's market position.

cards.title: Microsoft Cuts AI Costs With Own Models
cards.body: Microsoft joined other Silicon Valley giants in reducing AI spending. The company is increasingly relying on its proprietary models.

cards.title: Discord AI Moderation Bug Wrongfully Bans Users
cards.body: Discord's AI moderation incorrectly banned users over harmless images since May. The company fixed the bug after identifying 200 wrongful bans.

cards.title: Claude Cowork Expands to Mobile and Web
cards.body: Claude's AI collaboration tool now works seamlessly across desktop and mobile. Users can start tasks on one device and finish on another.

cards.title: Savi Protects Consumers From AI Voice Scams
cards.body: Savi raised $7 million to combat realistic AI-generated ransom scams. The app launches on iPhone and Android this week.

cards.title: AI-Run Ransomware Attack Still Needed Humans
cards.body: An AI agent executed a ransomware attack technically, but humans selected the victim and supplied credentials. The attack demonstrates partial AI autonomy in cybercrime.

cards.title: SK Hynix IPO Rides AI Memory Boom Wave
cards.body: SK Hynix is experiencing explosive growth driven by AI demand for memory chips. The South Korean company's multi-billion dollar U.S. IPO is imminent.

cards.title: Vercel CEO Discusses AI Models vs Agents Split
cards.body: Vercel's CEO highlights the importance of optimizing AI models and agents separately for production. Price and performance considerations drive this architectural approach.

cards.title: Treasury Warns on AI, OpenAI Wealth Sharing
cards.body: Sam Altman's proposal for Americans to share AI-generated wealth resurfaces in policy discussions. Treasury officials are examining the implications.

cards.title: IT Leaders Must Scale AI Architecture Foundations
cards.body: Organizations are expanding AI use cases and moving toward agentic systems. IT leaders face challenges scaling infrastructure while managing emerging risks.

cards.title: Android Bench Updated With New LLM Tests
cards.body: Google updated Android Bench to include new language model benchmarks. Gemini still lags behind competing AI models in performance.

cards.title: Hackers Exploit Popular AI Tools for Botnets
cards.body: Nine of the most popular AI tools can be exploited to build massive botnets. The discovery reveals significant security vulnerabilities in AI platforms.

cards.title: China's DeepSeek Plans Domestic AI Chip Production
cards.body: Facing U.S. export controls, DeepSeek is developing its own semiconductor chips. The move aims to maintain AI development capabilities independently.
